methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $2.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B+

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Electric Hydrogen is a Series C climate and energy technology company founded by Raffi Garabedian and David Eaglesham. The target has raised $380M to date against a stated post-money valuation exceeding $1.0B. The valuation assessment draws from a peer universe of nine private companies and one public comparable (LNZA), all operating in the climate and energy sector at similar development stages.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchored to the $1.0B last post-money and applying a typical 1.7x Series C advancement multiplier, implies $1.7B. Peer-median analysis examined three same-sector, same-stage comparables—KoBold Metals at $2.0B, Watershed at $1.8B, and Amogy at $700M—producing a median valuation of $1.8B and a range spanning $700M to $2.0B.

Triangulation across these two methods supports a valuation range of $1.4B (low) to $2.1B (high), with a median of $1.8B. The relatively tight clustering between stage-progression ($1.7B) and peer-median ($1.8B) results provides modest confidence in the midpoint estimate. Key uncertainties include the disparate valuations within the peer set (Amogy at $700M versus KoBold at $2.0B) and the limited public market reference point, both of which widen confidence intervals around terminal value assessments for early-stage capital deployment models in this sector.
